findings.one_line: The proved upper bound uses ass:marginal-overlap and the feasible nuisance-rate/exponent conditions, but the displayed supremum is still over def:law-class P_class(alpha,gamma); as written this is only an upper bound on a stricter subclass unless the target class is narrowed explicitly.

This node presents a two-sided characterization of M_n(alpha,gamma), but its upper half inherits oeq:feasible-upper's extra feasible-side assumptions outside def:law-class, so the delivered content is a converse on P_class plus an achievability result for a narrower scoped class.

The lemma is marked proved but the proof only cites a standard Le Cam/testing inequality; no declared dependency or in-core derivation discharges the testing lower bound constant used in thm:minimax-lower.
